apply: Students will be able to apply their knowledge of photosynthesis to explain various phenomena such as the water cycle, food chain, and plant growth.
Students will be able to use their understanding of the light-dependent and light-independent reactions to solve problems related to photosynthesis.
Students will be able to design an experiment to investigate the effect of light intensity on photosynthesis.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the factors that affect photosynthesis and their impact on plant growth and development.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the photosynthetic processes in different plants and environments.
Students will be able to evaluate the importance of photosynthesis in maintaining the balance of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the atmosphere.
remember: Students will be able to recall the overall process of photosynthesis in higher plants.
Students will be able to remember the key components of the light-dependent and light-independent reactions.
Students will be able to recall the role of pigments and stomata in photosynthesis.
understand: Students will be able to understand the significance of ATP and NADPH in the light-dependent reactions.
Students will be able to comprehend the role of RuBisCO in the Calvin cycle.
Students will be able to explain the importance of light intensity and CO2 concentration in photosynthesis.

ncert_objectives: Explain the process of photosynthesis in higher plants.
Describe the light-dependent reactions and the light-independent reactions.
Recall the role of pigments, stomata, and other structures in photosynthesis.
Explain the significance of ATP and NADPH in the light-dependent reactions.
Describe the role of RuBisCO in the Calvin cycle.
board_exam_objectives: Explain the overall process of photosynthesis in higher plants.
Describe the light-dependent and light-independent reactions.
Calculate the amount of glucose produced during photosynthesis.
Explain the factors that affect photosynthesis and their impact on plant growth and development.
Describe the importance of photosynthesis in maintaining the balance of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the atmosphere.
